title: salvia triloba subsp. cypria
intro: Salvia triloba subsp. cypria, also known as the Cypriot fringed sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the island of Cyprus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Salvia triloba subsp. cypria include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Salvia triloba subsp. cypria can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible medicinal herb.
